title: ECOGEO 'Omics Training: 3.1 Amplicon Analysis - Mothur
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7a0aq","text":"mothur is a bioinformatics tool for analyzing 16S rRNA gene sequences, which can be used to process data generated by Sanger, PacBio, IonTorrent, 454, and Illumina (MiSeq/HiSeq). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fpate","text":"Before starting, please visit the ECOGEO website for more information on this \"Introduction to Environmental 'Omics\" training series. The site contains a pre-packaged virtual machine that can be downloaded and used to run all of the protocols in this protocols.io collection. In addition to the VM, the website contains video and presentations from our initial \"Intro to Env 'Omics\" workshop held at the Univ. of Hawai'i at Manoa on 25-26 Jul 2016.\n\nPlease email ‘ecogeo-join@earthcube.org’ to join the ECOGEO listserv for future updates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Move to directory containing mothur and launch mothur $ cd /home/c-debi/ecogeo/mothurdir$ mothur $ cd /home/c-debi/ecogeo/mothurdir $ mothur
To perform rarefaction, open a second terminal window and navigate to /mothurdir. Less output file: mothur >
Creates a file with data for rarefaction curve (nano)

Alpha diversity measures: mothur > summary.single(shared=stability.an.shared, calc=nseqs-coverage-sobs-invsimpson, subsample=2441)$ less stability.an.groups.ave-std.summary If there is no mothur > summary.single(shared=stability.an.shared, calc=nseqs-coverage-sobs-invsimpson, subsample=2441) $ less stability.an.groups.ave-std.summary If there is no "$" at the beginning of the command, perform it in mothur.

What about bar graphs with relative abundance and taxonomy? 0.03.subsample.shared file was missing. Should be fixed in VM 2.0 release $ less less stability.an.cons.taxonomy$ less stability.an.0.03.subsample.shared $ less less stability.an.cons.taxonomy $ less stability.an.0.03.subsample.shared

Turn distance measure output in to a dendrogram: mothur > tree.shared(phylip=stability.an.thetayc.0.03.lt.ave.dist)mothur > quit()$ less stability.an.thetayc.0.03.lt.ave.tre$ Dendroscope Copy content of less before launching Dendroscope mothur > tree.shared(phylip=stability.an.thetayc.0.03.lt.ave.dist) mothur > quit() $ less stability.an.thetayc.0.03.lt.ave.tre $ Dendroscope Copy content of less before launching Dendroscope
creator: Elisha M Wood-Charlson
